results.title: Seoul Subway app revamped to improve access for foreign riders
results.description: SEOUL: Seoul Metro said on Tuesday (May 19) it has overhauled its Seoul Subway mobile app to help foreign residents and tourists navigate the city's subway system with ease by expanding language support, including for live updates.

results.title: Narragansett teacher earns national James Madison Fellowship
results.description: Mary Remillard of Narragansett Regional High School has been awarded a James Madison Fellowship for teaching the U.S. Constitution.

results.title: BiH Election Commission signs KM 74.5 million deal for biometric voter ID and ballot scanning
results.description: SARAJEVO, May 19 (FENA) – The President of the Central Election Commission (CIK) of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Jovan Kalaba, and the director of the company Smartmatic BH, Frans Gunink, representing the consortium of bidders, signed a contract worth KM 74,527,455.84 for the procurement of a biometric voter identification system and ballot scanning system.

results.title: Fintech vs banks: Zenith, UBA, 2 others dominate electronic transactions market
results.description: Major Nigerian banks processed N570.17 trillion in electronic transactions in 2025, with Zenith Bank and UBA leading amidst rising fintech competition.

results.title: No Plan to Use Temple Gold for Monetisation, Says Govt
results.description: It also clarified that claims suggesting gold plates on temple towers, doors, or other temple structures will be considered as 'Strategic Gold Reserves of India' are false, misleading, and entirely baseless.

results.title: Baby Cheramy partners with German Lanka Friendship Hospital, Galle to uplift maternity care
results.description: Baby Cheramy, Sri Lanka’s most trusted baby care brand, is pleased to announce that, as part of its purpose to support mothers with more holistic childcare, the brand has successfully implemented a special initiative in partnership with the German Sri Lanka Friendship Hospital in Galle. As one of the leading healthcare institutions in the Southern Province, caring for a significant number of expectant mothers and newborns, this initiative was designed to enhance the overall experience of parents visiting the hospital. Through this initiative, Baby Cheramy introduced a series of directional and informational signages across the hospital to help visitors navigate with ease while accessing essential maternal and infant care information. Presented in all three national languages, these signages offer guidance on pregnancy, childbirth, and early childcare, empowering parents to make informed decisions throughout their journey. As part of its extended commitment, Baby Cheramy also initiated a one-year partnership to support the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU) with a donation of newborn diapers. The first phase, comprising a three-month supply of diapers, was handed over on January 30th, 2026, in the presence of Hospital Director Dr. K. M. M. Somarathna; Nursing Officer (NICU) S. W. A. F. Senani; Special Grade Nursing Officer T. K. P. P. Dias; and Medical Officer Dr. M. A. T. I. Wickramasinghe. Designed to meet the highest standards of safety and comfort, Baby Cheramy newborn diapers are well-suited even for the delicate needs of preterm babies, reinforcing the brand’s commitment to infant hygiene and wellbeing from the very first day of life- especially in critical care environments. Commenting on the initiative, Mr. Shiyan Jayaweera, Director – Marketing, Hemas Consumer Brands, said, “The project including the NICU diaper support reflects Baby Cheramy’s long-standing purpose of creating a safer world through better access, education and solutions for holistic childcare for Sri Lankan mothers and babies. For over six decades, the brand has supported new mothers with trusted products and educational initiatives that promote better baby care and child-rearing practices.” Dr. Somarathne Konara, Director of the German Sri Lanka Friendship Hospital, commented on the collaboration: “This project, along with the generous donation of newborn diapers to our Neonatal Intensive Care Unit, has significantly enhanced the hospital experience for expectant parents and visitors.” He added that the clear, informative signage provides practical guidance, while the educational content supports new mothers in key areas of care. “We highly value this partnership with Baby Cheramy, which helps us advance our shared goal of promoting the health and well-being of mothers and infants,” he said. This latest collaboration between Baby Cheramy and the German Sri Lanka Friendship Hospital in Galle is another testament to the brand’s historical commitment to raising generations of Sri Lankans by empowering safe motherhood, educated parenting, and enhanced early childcare practices. Partnering with South Asia’s largest and most advanced maternity hospital, featuring over 640 beds, specialised maternal and neonatal care facilities, six operating theatres, emergency treatment units, an ICU, Neonatal ICU, and dedicated paediatric units, along with the capacity to serve more than 800 patients through its OPD and clinics, further reinforces this shared vision of elevating maternal and child healthcare outcomes. As Baby Cheramy continues to champion safer beginnings through meaningful initiatives, the brand remains steadfast in its purpose of ‘Creating a safer world for our babies’. Photo Caption: Baby Cheramy handed over a one-year supply of newborn diapers to the NICU of the German Sri Lanka Friendship Hospital, Galle, reaffirming the brand’s commitment to infant health and hygiene in critical care settings, with representatives from both Baby Cheramy and the hospital also pictured at the facility’s main entrance
